decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures with the Madness ability, using various spells to protect them and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells, while also having the flexibility to react to different board states. The deck sacrifices some consistency for explosiveness, using its graveyard as a resource to gain an advantage in the early game and maintain pressure throughout the match.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with land destruction spells to disrupt the opponent's mana base. By utilizing cards like Terravore and Armageddon, the deck aims to gain an advantage by limiting the opponent's resources while maintaining a strong board presence. Cards like Meddling Mage and Weathered Wayfarer help control the opponent's options, while Sylvan Library provides card advantage. The deck aims to pressure the opponent early, disrupt their game plan, and close out the game quickly before they can recover.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing cards like Terravore, Sphere of Resistance, and Winter's Grasp to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Oath of Druids is used to bring out powerful creatures quickly, while Sylvan Library helps to draw cards and maintain card advantage. The land base is focused on mana acceleration and disruption with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port. Overall, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources and controlling the game with removal spells and discard effects. It also includes recursion and value cards to generate long-term advantage. The strategy revolves around disrupting the opponent's game plan, while establishing a steady stream of threats and card advantage to grind out a victory. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent over the course of a game by efficiently removing threats and maintaining a steady flow of card advantage.

The main idea of this Aluren combo deck is to quickly get out Aluren on the battlefield and start chaining together free creature spells using its ability. The deck includes several low-cost creatures like Birds of Paradise, Cavern Harpy, and Raven Familiar to take advantage of this combo. Cards like Wall of Blossoms and Spike Feeder provide additional utility, while cards like Cabal Therapy and Pernicious Deed help control the board and disrupt the opponent's plans. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of creatures while disrupting their strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing enchantresses to draw cards and ramping up mana with enchantments like Wild Growth and Exploration. The key strategy is to assemble a powerful enchantment combo with Opalescence, turning all enchantments into creatures and potentially wiping out opponents with Parallax Wave. The deck also includes cards like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing for removal and disruption. The mana base is designed to support the enchantress strategy, with Serra's Sanctum providing additional mana for casting multiple enchantments in a turn.

This deck focuses on disrupting your opponent's plans through discard spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Snuff Out and Smother. The win condition is to slowly chip away at your opponent's life total using cards like The Rack and Hypnotic Specter, all while maintaining a resource advantage with cards like Skeletal Scrying and Phyrexian Furnace. The deck also includes land destruction elements with Wasteland and Rishadan Port to further disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to heavily control the game through counterspells and bounce spells, while using lands like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave as win conditions. Standstill and Parallax Tide can help stall the opponent while accumulating card advantage through card draw spells like Impulse and Accumulated Knowledge. Additionally, the deck includes removal in the form of Nevinyrral's Disk and Powder Keg to deal with threats. The goal is to outlast the opponent and slowly grind them down while maintaining control of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize enchantments to generate card advantage and ramp, while also being able to create a combo with Opalescence and Parallax Wave to control the board and potentially win the game. The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Exploration, while drawing cards with Enchantress's Presence and Mirri's Guile. The combo pieces of Opalescence and Parallax Wave can create a lock on the opponent's board, allowing for a powerful win condition.

This deck focuses on aggressively deploying and buffing goblin creatures to overwhelm the opponent. With key cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron, the deck can quickly establish a threatening board presence. Goblin Piledriver and Goblin Warchief enhance the offensive power of the goblins, while creature removal cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander help control the opponent's board. The inclusion of cards like Naturalize ensures the deck can handle problematic enchantments or artifacts. The mana base is tailored to support the goblin-centric strategy with a mix of Mountains, Forest','','');">Karplusan Forest, Rishadan Port, and Wooded Foothills.

The primary focus of this deck is to control the tempo of the game by slowing down opponent's resources with cards like Winter Orb, Tangle Wire, and Sphere of Resistance. The deck also aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port. Mishra's Factory provides a resilient threat while Karn, Silver Golem and Masticore offer additional control options. The deck utilizes artifact acceleration with cards like Metalworker and Thran Dynamo to power out its threats and spells quickly. Ultimately, the goal is to outlast the opponent and grind out a victory through attrition.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Oath of Druids enchantment to quickly get Terravore onto the battlefield, supported by disruptive control elements like Swords to Plowshares and Mana Leak. The deck also includes cards like Call of the Herd to provide additional threats and Cataclysm to disrupt opponents' board states. The mana base is built to support the deck's game plan while also including lands like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to control the game while setting up powerful creatures to win the game quickly.

The main focus of this Mono Red Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and win through consistent damage. By using cards like Stone Rain, Pillage, and Avalanche Riders to destroy lands, combined with Ankh of Mishra and Sulfuric Vortex to punish opponents for playing spells or lands, this deck aims to control the game while slowly wearing down the opponent's life total. The inclusion of burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate provide additional reach, while cards like Tangle Wire and Grim Lavamancer help to stall the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to drop a Phyrexian Dreadnought onto the battlefield, then quickly follow it up with a Stifle to counter the 'enters the battlefield' trigger and ensure it stays there. This combo allows for a quick and powerful creature to hit the board, overwhelming the opponent. The deck also includes card draw and countermagic to protect the combo, as well as alternative win conditions like Psychatog and Wonder. The mana base is focused on islands with some black sources for support.

The deck is focused on quickly ramping up mana through cards like Birds of Paradise, Mox Diamond, and Gemstone Mine to play disruptive creatures like Terravore and Weathered Wayfarer. It also includes powerful spells like Armageddon to disrupt the opponent's mana base and Swords to Plowshares for removal. The deck aims to control the board and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. It also utilizes Survival of the Fittest to tutor for key creatures and maintain card advantage with Sylvan Library. The deck has a strong land destruction theme with Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's resources.

The main concept of this Aggro White Weenie deck is to utilize low-cost creatures and efficient removal spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with early aggression. By playing a consistent stream of cheap creatures like Mother of Runes, Ramosian Sergeant, and Steadfast Guard, combined with disruption from cards like Glowrider and Whipcorder, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence. Additionally, using powerful white removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Abolish, along with land disruption from Wasteland, the deck is designed to disrupt the opponent's game plan while pushing for a quick victory.
